title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
summary_zh: 心脏传导系统：窦房结（SA结）作为天然起搏器控制心率，电信号经房室结（AV结）和束支传导至心室，这些通路统称为心脏传导系统。
text_en: The heart has a natural pacemaker called the Sinoatrial node or SA node. The pacemaker controls the heart rate by precisely determining when the muscles of the atria and ventricles contract. The electrical signal produced by the SA node reaches the ventricles through a narrow pathway that includes the so-called AV node and bundle branches. Taken together, these pathways are referred to as the conduction system of the heart.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
summary_zh: 传导系统异常：窦房结功能障碍可导致心跳过快、过慢或不规则；束支传导阻滞可使心跳变慢或不规则。这些问题可由心脏病、药物或心脏手术引起。
text_en: Problems with the conduction system can result if the SA node malfunctions, causing the heart to beat too fast, too slow or irregularly. Alternatively, a blockage in the bundle branches can develop, causing the heart to beat more slowly or irregularly. These problems can be due to heart disease, medications, or cardiac surgery.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
summary_zh: 术前准备：进行血液和尿液检查、胸部X光检查、心电图（EKG）记录心脏电活动，并在植入部位注射局部麻醉剂。
text_en: Before the procedure, your blood and urine will be tested. You will also have x-rays taken of your chest, and an electrocardiogram or EKG will record the electrical activity in your heart. A local anesthetic will be injected into your skin to numb the area where the pacemaker will be inserted.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
summary_zh: 起搏器植入手术过程：在锁骨下方做小切口，将导线经锁骨下静脉送入右心房，通过X光影像引导定位；测试导线后连接脉冲发生器，将其放入皮下囊袋中，最后缝合切口。
text_en: The surgeon will make a small incision beneath your collarbone. The pacemaker's leads will be inserted into the subclavian vein, and then threaded toward your heart until they reach the right atrium. Your surgeon will view x-ray images on a video screen to position the leads correctly. Once the leads have been tested, they will be connected to the pulse generator. The pulse generator will then be placed in a small pouch that your surgeon will create under the skin beneath your collarbone. Your surgeon will then sew the incision back together.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
summary_zh: 术后恢复与随访：住院期间监测心率和血压以确认起搏器正常工作，出院前调整起搏器参数；术后可用非处方止痛药缓解疼痛，约两周内不能驾车，需定期复诊检查和调整起搏器。
text_en: While you're in the hospital, your doctor will monitor your heart rate and blood pressure to be certain the pacemaker is working properly. Before you leave, he or she will make final adjustments to the pacemaker settings. After your procedure, over the counter pain medications such as acetaminophen or ibuprofen should be sufficient to relieve any pain. You will not be able to drive for about two weeks. Periodically, you will need to follow up with your doctor to test and adjust your new

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 主动脉解剖结构：主动脉是人体最大的动脉，连接心脏左心室，分为升主动脉、主动脉弓、胸主动脉和腹主动脉四部分。
text_en: The largest artery in the body is the aorta, which is attached to the heart and receives blood directly from the left ventricle or main pumping chamber. The aorta is divided into four parts, the ascending aorta, aortic arch, thoracic aorta, and the abdominal aorta.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 腹主动脉的功能与结构：腹主动脉是主动脉最后也是最大的部分，向腹部和盆腔器官及下肢供血，在脐部水平分为两条髂动脉；主动脉壁厚实，能承受心脏泵血的高压。
text_en: The abdominal aorta is the last and largest section of the aorta. It supplies blood to the lower regions of your body, including the organs in the abdomen and pelvis. The abdominal aorta ends around naval level, where it splits into the two iliac arteries that carry blood to the legs. The aorta has a thick wall so it can withstand the high pressure of blood being pumped from the heart.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 腹主动脉瘤的形成：随着时间推移，主动脉壁可能变弱，像气球一样向外膨出，当这种情况发生在腹部区域时称为腹主动脉瘤。
text_en: Over time, the wall of the aorta may weaken, slowly bulging outward like a balloon. When this condition occurs in the abdominal region, it is called an abdominal aortic aneurysm.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 开放手术适应证：当主动脉直径超过两英寸，或出现剧烈腹痛、异常腹部搏动等症状时，外科医生将进行开放式腹主动脉瘤修复。
text_en: Your surgeon will perform an open abdominal aortic aneurysm repair when the diameter of your aorta exceeds two inches, or if you're having symptoms such as intense abdominal pain, or an abnormally prominent abdominal pulsation.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 开放手术术前准备：建立静脉通路输注液体、抗生素和麻醉药；全身麻醉下插入气管插管辅助呼吸；插入导尿管监测肾功能。
text_en: Before the open procedure an IV line will be started to provide you with fluids, antibiotics, and anesthesia. A traditional open surgical repair is performed under general anesthesia, which means you will be asleep for the duration of the procedure. A breathing tube will be inserted through your mouth and into your windpipe to help you breathe during the operation. When you're asleep, a catheter will be inserted into your bladder to drain your urine and monitor your kidney function.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 开放手术操作过程：从胸骨至脐下做切口，定位动脉瘤并在其上下钳夹主动脉，切开动脉瘤缝合人工移植物，将动脉瘤壁缝合覆盖移植物以加固主动脉壁，最后缝合腹部切口，手术通常耗时4-6小时。
text_en: Your surgeon will begin by making an incision from the breastbone to below the navel. He or she will then locate the aneurysm and clamp the aorta just above and below it. Your surgeon will then open the aneurysm and sew an artificial graft, the same size and shape as a healthy aorta, into place. Your surgeon will stitch the wall of the aneurysm closed over the newly placed graft. The graft will reinforce the walls of the aorta. Your surgeon will close the abdominal incision with stitches. The total length of this procedure is usually four to six hours.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 血管内修复术适应证与术前准备：适用于严重心脏病或因年龄等原因手术风险较高的患者；建立静脉通路，可选全麻或局部麻醉（镇静剂+硬膜外/区域麻醉）。
text_en: Your surgeon will perform endovascular aneurysm repair, a newer, minimally invasive procedure, if you have severe heart disease or are at increased surgical risk due to age or other medical conditions. Before the endovascular procedure, as with the open procedure, an IV line will be started to provide you with fluids and antibiotics. You may receive general anesthesia or you may receive a sedative and an epidural or regional anesthesia to numb only the lower half of your body.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 血管内修复术操作过程：在腹股沟股动脉处做小切口，插入导丝推进至动脉瘤部位，通过导管在X线引导下输送压缩的支架移植物至动脉瘤处并展开，用金属钩固定；可插入额外移植物组件延伸至下肢血管；血流通过移植物流动，不再进入动脉瘤囊，解除动脉瘤压力；最后撤除导管缝合切口，手术约2-3小时。
text_en: Your surgeon will begin by making a small incision in your groin over your femoral artery. Then he or she will insert a guide wire into the artery and gently push it up toward the site of your aneurysm. A catheter or hollow tube will be passed over the guide wire and pushed toward your aneurysm. During live x-ray pictures as a guide, your surgeon will pass an endovascular stent graft, a compressed fabric and metal tube, through the catheter to the aneurysm. Then he or she will open the graft which will be held in place with metallic hooks and Your surgeon may insert additional graft components to extend the graft into the blood vessels supplying your legs. Once the graft is in place, your blood will flow through it, not into the surrounding aneurysm sac which will remove the pressure from your aneurysm. Finally, your surgeon will remove the catheters and close the incisions in your groin. The procedure will generally take 2-3 hours.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 术后恢复：拔除气管插管后转入ICU，通过静脉输液补充营养，可能经鼻插胃管引流分泌物直至肠道恢复功能；开放手术住院5-10天，血管内修复术住院2-3天。
text_en: After your procedure, if you had a breathing tube, it will be removed and you will be taken to the intensive care unit for recovery. You will receive fluids and nutrition through your IV. You may also have a tube inserted through your nose and into your stomach to remove secretions until your intestines regain normal functioning. The usual hospital stay for an open procedure is 5-10 days, while the stay for an endovascular procedure is usually shorter at 2-3 days.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
summary_zh: 血液的基本功能：运输氧气和营养物质、带走二氧化碳和废物、抵抗感染和愈合伤口
text_en: Your blood carries oxygen and nutrients to all of your tissues and removes carbon dioxide and other waste products from them. Blood also helps fight infection and heal wounds.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
summary_zh: 血液的组成成分：液体部分（血浆，含营养素、激素、凝血因子等）和细胞成分（红细胞运输氧气和二氧化碳、白细胞抵抗感染、血小板辅助凝血）
text_en: Blood consists of a liquid portion and cellular components. The liquid called plasma is where nutrients, hormones, clotting factors and other chemicals are dissolved. The cellular components are suspended in the plasma. They consist of red blood cells which transport oxygen and carbon dioxide. White blood cells which fight infection. Blood cell and platelets which are small subcells that help the blood to clot.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
summary_zh: 血细胞在骨髓中生成；输血利用供体血液补充红细胞、白细胞、血小板、凝血因子、血浆或全血
text_en: Blood cells are made in the bone marrow, a soft tissue housed inside many of your bones. A blood transfusion uses donor blood to replenish red blood cells, white blood cells, platelets, clotting factors, plasma or whole blood.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
summary_zh: 需要输血的常见情况：创伤失血、心脏或大手术、器官移植、血友病等出血性疾病、严重贫血（含镰状细胞贫血）、白血病或其他癌症治疗、骨髓破坏性疾病、新生儿Rh血型不合
text_en: Some stances that may require a blood transfusion include blood loss due to trauma, heart or other major surgery, organ transplants, bleeding disorders such as hemophilia, severe anemia including sickle cell anemia, treatment for leukemia or other types of cancer, disorders that destroy blood cells or bone marrow or RH incompatibility in newborn babies.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
summary_zh: 输血操作过程：在手部静脉插入静脉针，悬挂供体血袋，血液通过管路缓慢滴入静脉进入血液循环；全程监测体温、心率、呼吸和血压
text_en: To begin the transfusion an intravenous needle will be inserted into a vein in your hand A bag containing the donor blood will be hung nearby and the blood will drip slowly through a tube attached to the IV needle, allowing it to run through your vein into your bloodstream. Throughout the transfusion your temperature, heart rate, breathing and blood pressure will be monitored.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
summary_zh: 输血完成与术后处理：血袋用完可更换新袋；输血结束后拔除针头；医生监测生命体征以排查不良反应；必要时使用利尿剂减轻水肿或液体潴留
text_en: After the bag is empty if you require more blood, another bag will be attached to the When your transfusion is completed, the needle will be removed. After your transfusion, your doctor will monitor your vital signs to make sure you're not having an adverse reaction. If necessary, you may be given a diuretic to reduce any swelling or fluid retention.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
summary_zh: 介绍电复律的定义：当患者出现心律异常时，医生通过电击恢复正常心跳节律。
text_en: If you have an abnormal heart rhythm, your doctor may perform electrical cardioversion, where he or she uses an electrical shock to restore your normal heart beat.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
summary_zh: 正常心脏电传导系统：心脏正常以60-100次/分的规律节律跳动，电信号起源于右心房壁的窦房结（SA结），传导至心肌引起收缩泵血。
text_en: The heart normally beats in a regular rhythm at 60 to 100 beats per minute. This steady and coordinated rhythm allows the heart to effectively pump blood. The electrical signals that travel through the heart control the heart's rhythm. These electrical signals start in a cluster of cells called the Sinoatrial node or pacemaker. The Sinoatrial node or SA node is located in the wall of the right upper chamber of the heart, called the right atrium. When the electrical signals reach the heart muscle, they cause it to contract and pump blood.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
summary_zh: 房颤及其并发症：房颤时心房因混乱电信号而颤动，导致心跳异常加快或不规则；血液在心房内淤积可形成血栓，血栓进入循环系统可导致中风。
text_en: An abnormal heart rhythm is called an arrhythmia. During atrial fibrillation, the atria quiver because of chaotic electrical signals in the heart, resulting in an abnormally fast or irregular heartbeat. Because of the abnormal heartbeat, blood may pool in the atria, which can result in the formation of a blood clot. If the blood clot passes into the general circulation, it may cause a stroke.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
summary_zh: 电复律手术操作流程：建立静脉通路并镇静，放置心电图电极监测心脏电活动，在胸部放置复律电极片或电极板，通过电击使心脏恢复正常节律，必要时重复电击。
text_en: Before the procedure, you will be connected to an intravenous line. You will be sedated so you will sleep through the procedure. EKG electrodes will be placed on your chest to monitor your heart's electrical activity. Once you are sedated, special cardioversion pads or paddles will be placed on the skin of your chest or your chest and back. These pads or paddles will deliver electrical shock through your chest to your heart. The shock may be repeated several times until the heart resumes its normal rhythm.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
summary_zh: 术后恢复：患者在恢复室或冠心病监护病房接受密切监测，待镇静消退且病情稳定后出院。
text_en: After your electrical cardioversion, you will be monitored closely in a recovery room or in the coronary care unit of the hospital. Once the sedation wears off and you are in stable condition, you will be set home.

title: ANH00023 Non-cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (NCPTA)
summary_zh: 动脉粥样硬化与血管狭窄的病理机制：脂肪沉积形成粥样硬化斑块，堵塞动脉通道，导致血流受阻（狭窄/stenosis），引起组织缺血疼痛
text_en: transliminal angioplasty, or NCPTA, to restore blood flow in an artery blocked by an atherosclerotic plaque. Fatty deposits called atherosclerotic plaques can build up inside your arteries, narrowing the passageway and interfering with blood flow. This arterial blockage is called stenosis. Insufficient blood flow to a tissue generally causes pain. A non-cardiac percutaneous

title: ANH00023 Non-cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (NCPTA)
summary_zh: 手术适应症与术前说明：NCPTA仅在生活方式改变和药物治疗等保守治疗无效时才进行；患者被置于配有透视机的手术台上
text_en: transliminal angioplasty is only done when your condition has not responded to less invasive treatment options, such as lifestyle changes and medications. To begin the procedure, you'll

title: ANH00023 Non-cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (NCPTA)
summary_zh: 血管穿刺与导管置入过程：透视机可多角度拍摄X光影像；医生在皮肤局部麻醉后，通常经腹股沟股动脉穿刺，通过空心针插入带球囊的导管，并在透视引导下推进导管至目标动脉
text_en: be placed on a table equipped with a fluoroscope. A fluoroscope is capable of taking x-ray images of many parts of the body from multiple angles. Your doctor will numb your skin around where the catheter is to be inserted. Since the arteries of the leg are most easily accessed, the femoral artery in the groin serves as the insertion site in most cases. Your doctor will initially gain access to the artery by inserting a hollow needle through which the balloon-tipped catheter is passed. Watching on the fluoroscopy screen, your doctor will gently advance the catheter until it

title: ANH00023 Non-cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (NCPTA)
summary_zh: 造影定位与球囊扩张过程：注入造影剂拍摄血管造影图，精确定位阻塞位置；将球囊定位于狭窄处，反复快速充气放气，将斑块和血管壁向外推开，部分恢复血流
text_en: is positioned in the artery being treated and eject a special die. This die allows the fluoroscope to take x-ray images called angiograms, which lets your doctor identify the exact location of the blockages. Next, your doctor will move the catheter until the deflated balloon at its tip is positioned at the site of the stenosis. Then, by rapidly inflating and deflating the balloon, your doctor will gently push the plaque and vessel wall out, partially reestablishing blood flow.

title: ANH00023 Non-cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (NCPTA)
summary_zh: 支架植入过程：对于额外阻塞可重复球囊扩张；部分病例需植入金属网状支架，将折叠支架套在球囊上送入血管，球囊充气时支架膨胀并锁定在血管壁上，保持血管通畅
text_en: This procedure may be repeated for any additional blockages. In some cases, your doctor may place a stent, which is a wire mesh that remains in the artery to hold it open. To perform this procedure, your doctor will place a collapsed stent over the deflated balloon on the tip of the catheter. When the balloon is inflated, the stent expands and locks into place.
